summary: The team convened to address fallout from a six-hour platform outage that caused a complete loss of threat monitoring visibility. Competitors SentinelShield and CyberNova are actively targeting at-risk accounts, including Hartfield Financial and prospect Clearvale Systems. The group aligned on a two-part response strategy: leading with a transparent post-mortem and remediation narrative now, followed by a Comply v2 launch touchpoint in early April to show forward momentum. Key concerns include a potential liability issue involving a customer who claims an actual security incident occurred during the outage window.
actionItems: Megan Lawson: Update competitive battlecards this week to reflect the outage response story and SentinelShield's own historical incident data
Brian Cho: Prepare a customer-facing technical summary of the incident and remediation steps by end of day Monday
James Mitchell: Reach out to Hartfield Financial early next week to schedule a call with their CISO above the procurement level
Daniel Okafor: Prepare a strong follow-up pitch for Clearvale Systems including potential enhanced SLA commitments to salvage the deal

keyMoments.text: James Mitchell reveals Hartfield Financial has already been contacted by competitor SentinelShield following the outage
keyMoments.speaker: James Mitchell

keyMoments.text: Brian Cho discloses a customer is claiming the outage may have delayed their response to an actual security incident, flagging a potential liability issue
keyMoments.speaker: Brian Cho

keyMoments.text: Tom Bradley outlines concrete remediation steps including redundant processing nodes, circuit breaker pattern, and weekly automated failover testing
keyMoments.speaker: Tom Bradley

keyMoments.text: James Mitchell suggests pairing the outage recovery narrative with the upcoming Comply v2 launch to give at-risk accounts a forward-looking reason to stay
keyMoments.speaker: James Mitchell
